task_completion.description: Search Grants.gov using the specified keyword.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Grants.gov website is open with 'rural health' entered in the search bar or displayed in the search results header.

task_completion.description: Apply the required eligibility filter for nonprofits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The eligibility filter for 'Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education' is checked or visibly applied in the Grants.gov search interface.

task_completion.description: Create a bookmark folder named 'Grant Prospects'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's bookmark manager, bookmark bar, or bookmark menu shows a folder explicitly named 'Grant Prospects'.

task_completion.description: Bookmark the 'View Opportunity' pages for three grants in the 'Grant Prospects' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Grant Prospects' bookmark folder is shown containing at least 3 saved links to distinct Grants.gov 'View Opportunity' pages.

task_completion.description: Compile the grant details into a JSON file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file viewer displays a file named 'grant_prospects.json' located in the Documents folder. The file contains valid JSON with an array of at least 3 grants, including the keys: opportunity_number, title, agency, posted_date, and close_date.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Grants.gov website.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser shows the authentic Grants.gov interface and URL, rather than a mocked local HTML file or text-based search.

integrity.description: The agent used the browser's actual bookmarking functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's native bookmark UI (e.g., Firefox Library, bookmark dropdown, or star icon menu) is used, rather than just writing a list of URLs in a text document.

integrity.description: The agent manually compiled the JSON file or used standard GUI tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, VS Code, or nano) is visible showing the JSON content being edited, rather than the file appearing magically via a pre-written background script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target keyword: 'rural health'. Required bookmark folder: 'Grant Prospects'. Required output file: '/home/ga/Documents/grant_prospects.json'. Minimum opportunities required: 3. The JSON file must contain the fields: opportunity_number, title, agency, posted_date, and close_date. Authentic grants for this keyword include Rural Health Transformation Program, Rural Health Care Services Outreach Program, and Rural Health Network Advancement Program.
